title: Extraction and spectrophotometric determination of molybdenum(V) as its thiocyanate complex in industrial, environmental and soil samples
author: A. Kumar
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Industrial Chemistry, Guru Nanak Khalsa College, Yamuna Nagar-135 001, Haryana, India 

 Department of Chemistry, Haryana Engineering College, Jagadhari-135 003, Haryana, India 

  Manuscript received 26 February 2008, revised 15 October 2008, accepted 12 November 2008  

 A simple and rapid extractive spectrophotometric method for micro determination of molybdenum using thiocyanate as a complexing agent has been worked out for industrial, environmental and soil samples. The Mo v - thioqanate complex is extracted into 2,2' -dipyridyl in chloroform. The vermilion colour of the extract is measured at λ max  505 nm against a similarly prepared reagent blank. The 1 : 4 (Mo : SCN) complex is found to be ~table for more than one hour in the extract and obeys Beer's law over the concentration range of 0-11 µg Mo ml -1 . The molar absorptivity and Sandell's sensitivity of the proposed method are found to be 1.24 x 10 4  dm 3  mol -1  cm -1  and 0.0075 µg Mo cm -2 , respectively. A large number of cations, anions and complexing agents of major analytical importance such as Re vll , Cr lll , VI , Cu ll  , Zr lv , Mn ll  , Ni ll , Fc ll , lll , W vl , U vl , V v  and platinum metals, sulphate, chloride, fluoride, bicarbonate, phosphate. tartrate and EDTA do not interfere. Only oxalate, acetate, hydrogen peroxide, tin, cobalt, bismuth, titanium, osmium and ruthenium interfered in the procedure. The method has been applied satisfactorily for the determination of molybdenum in varieties of samples.
